title: competent-search-thing plugin request (wire protocol v1)
description: The JSON payload the app sends to a plugin for one query: written to stdin for command plugins (stdin is then closed), POSTed as the body (Content-Type: application/json) for HTTP plugins. Source of truth: internal/plugin/schema.go (Request). This schema describes what plugin AUTHORS receive and can rely on; parse it leniently (tolerate added fields in future versions) and reject any 'v' other than 1.

v.description: Protocol version, always 1. Reject anything else.

query.description: The raw text as typed, including any trigger prefix or bang.

stripped.description: The query with the trigger prefix or bang removed and trimmed; usually what you want to parse.

gen.description: The query generation (monotonically increasing). Echo-free: purely informational for logging/correlation.

targeted.description: true when the query targeted this plugin via a bang ('!calc 2+2'); trigger gating was bypassed.

bang.description: The canonical (registered) bang name that targeted this plugin, or "" when not targeted.

settings.description: This plugin's opaque settings object from config.json (plugins.entries.<id>.settings), or {} when there is none. Always a JSON object.

requestContext.title: App context
requestContext.description: The app-context parts this plugin declared in its manifest 'context' list. Undeclared parts are omitted; when a plugin declares nothing, the whole 'context' field is absent. A declared part can still be absent when the platform could not provide it (e.g. no focused app known on Wayland).

focused_app.description: The application focused at hotkey press (manifest context 'focused').

running_apps.description: Visible running applications (manifest context 'running'); deduped by pid, capped at 64, sorted by name.

name.description: Application name (window class on X11, exe base name on Windows).

exe.description: Executable path; may be "" when unreadable (cross-user processes).

title.description: Window title; may be "" (always empty on macOS).

exec.description: RAW launch command in .desktop Exec syntax (quotes, %-field codes not yet stripped).

icon.description: Platform icon ref (a .desktop Icon= value on linux, the absolute .app bundle path on darwin; absent on windows). Informational -- plugins may ignore it.
